course.name: Claude Desktop Mastery
course.description: Take your AI skills to the next level! Learn to use Claude Desktop app with powerful integrations. Connect to GitHub, databases, and more. Final project: Connect your Claude Desktop to ClassroomNeo!
warnings: This course requires comfort with using a computer
You'll need to install software and edit configuration files
Recommended: Complete 'AI Foundations' first if you're new to AI

modules.title: Module 1: Getting Started with Claude Desktop
modules.description: Download, install, and set up Claude Desktop on your computer

steps.title: 1.1 Why Claude Desktop?
steps.content:
# Why Use Claude Desktop? 🖥️

## The Upgrade from Claude.ai

You've been using Claude.ai in your browser (great job!). Now let's level up!

### Claude Desktop Can:

🗂️ **Access Your Files**
- Read documents on your computer
- Work with multiple files at once
- No more copy-pasting!

🔌 **Connect to Tools**
- GitHub for code
- Databases for data
- And much more!

🚀 **Work Faster**
- Keyboard shortcuts
- Instant access
- Better for long work sessions

## Simple Comparison

| Feature | Claude.ai (Browser) | Claude Desktop |
|---------|---------------------|----------------|
| Use anywhere | ✅ Any device | ❌ Your computer only |
| Access local files | ❌ Upload only | ✅ Full access |
| Connect to tools | ❌ No | ✅ Yes! |
| Works offline | ❌ No | ⚠️ Partial |

## What You'll Learn

By the end of this course:
1. ✅ Install Claude Desktop
2. ✅ Connect to external tools
3. ✅ Work with GitHub
4. ✅ Build automated workflows
5. ✅ Connect to ClassroomNeo!

## Before We Start

Make sure you:
- Have completed **AI Foundations** course
- Have an Anthropic account (same as claude.ai)
- Have about 1 hour for installation

steps.title: 1.2 Installation (Step-by-Step)
steps.content:
# Installing Claude Desktop 📥

## Step 1: Download

1. Open your browser
2. Go to: **claude.ai/download**
3. Click the download button for your system

![Download page](https://example.com/placeholder)

---

## Step 2: Install

### 🍎 For Mac Users:

1. Find the downloaded file (usually in Downloads folder)
2. Double-click the `.dmg` file
3. A window opens - drag Claude to Applications
4. Done! Find Claude in your Applications folder

### 🪟 For Windows Users:

1. Find the downloaded `.exe` file
2. Double-click to run the installer
3. Follow the on-screen instructions
4. Click "Finish" when done
5. Find Claude in Start Menu

---

## Step 3: First Launch

1. Open Claude Desktop
2. Sign in with your Anthropic account
   - Same account you use for claude.ai!
3. You should see the chat interface

---

## Step 4: Quick Test

Type this to Claude:
```
Hello! Can you confirm Claude Desktop is working?
```

If Claude responds, you're all set! 🎉

---

## ❓ Having Problems?

**Mac: "App can't be opened"**
- Right-click the app → Open → Click "Open" again

**Windows: "Windows protected your PC"**
- Click "More info" → "Run anyway"

**Can't sign in?**
- Make sure you have an Anthropic account
- Try signing in at claude.ai first

## ✅ Assignment

Take a screenshot showing:
1. Claude Desktop is open
2. You've sent a message
3. Claude has responded

Submit your screenshot to complete this step!

steps.title: 1.3 Getting to Know the Interface
steps.content:
# The Claude Desktop Interface 🎯

## Main Parts of the Screen

### 1. Sidebar (Left Side)
📂 **Conversations** - Your chat history
📁 **Projects** - Organize related chats
⚙️ **Settings** - Configuration options

### 2. Chat Area (Center)
💬 Where you talk to Claude
📎 Drag files here to share them

### 3. Input Box (Bottom)
⌨️ Type your messages here
📎 Click the paperclip to attach files

---

## Keyboard Shortcuts You'll Love

### Mac Users:
| What | Shortcut |
|------|----------|
| New chat | ⌘ + N |
| Settings | ⌘ + , |
| Search | ⌘ + K |

### Windows Users:
| What | Shortcut |
|------|----------|
| New chat | Ctrl + N |
| Settings | Ctrl + , |
| Search | Ctrl + K |

---

## Try These Now!

### 1. Start a New Chat
Press the shortcut for new chat.

### 2. Open Settings
Press the shortcut for settings.
Look around - you'll see options for:
- Appearance (dark/light mode)
- Model selection
- MCP Servers (we'll learn this next!)

### 3. Drag a File
Find any document on your computer.
Drag it into the chat area.
Ask Claude: "What is this file about?"

---

## Pro Tips 💡

1. **Use Projects** to organize chats by topic
2. **Star important chats** for quick access
3. **Dark mode** is easier on the eyes at night

## ✅ You're Ready!

Now you know your way around Claude Desktop.

Next up: **The Magic of MCP Servers!**
This is where Claude Desktop becomes incredibly powerful.

modules.title: Module 2: MCP - Connecting Claude to Tools
modules.description: Learn how MCP (Model Context Protocol) lets Claude connect to external tools and services

steps.title: 2.1 What is MCP? (Simple Explanation)
steps.content:
# What is MCP? 🔌

## Think of It Like a Universal Adapter

You know how you need different adapters for different countries?

MCP is like a **universal adapter** that lets Claude talk to many different tools!

---

## Without MCP:
```
You → Claude → Response
```
Claude can only chat.

## With MCP:
```
You → Claude → [MCP Adapter] → GitHub, Database, Files, etc.
```
Claude can DO things!

---

## Real Examples

### Example 1: File Access
**Without MCP:**
```
You: "Read the file project-notes.txt on my computer"
Claude: "I can't access files on your computer."
```

**With MCP:**
```
You: "Read the file project-notes.txt on my computer"
Claude: "Here's what's in that file: ..."
```

### Example 2: GitHub
**Without MCP:**
```
You: "Commit this code to my repo"
Claude: "I can't access GitHub."
```

**With MCP:**
```
You: "Commit this code to my repo"
Claude: "Done! I've committed the code."
```

---

## What MCP Servers Are Available?

🗂️ **Filesystem** - Read/write files on your computer
🐙 **GitHub** - Manage code repositories
🗄️ **Database** - Query databases
📁 **Google Drive** - Access cloud files
💬 **Slack** - Send messages
...and many more!

---

## How Hard Is It?

Setting up MCP requires:
1. Editing a configuration file (we'll guide you step-by-step!)
2. Installing Node.js (one-time setup)
3. Restarting Claude Desktop

It sounds technical, but we'll break it down into simple steps! 💪

steps.title: 2.2 Installing Node.js (Required)
steps.content:
# Installing Node.js 📦

## What is Node.js?

Think of it as a "translator" that helps MCP servers run on your computer.

You only need to install it once!

---

## 🍎 Mac Installation

### Option A: Download (Easiest)

1. Go to: **nodejs.org**
2. Click the **LTS** button (the one that says "Recommended")
3. Open the downloaded file
4. Follow the installer steps
5. Done!

### Option B: Using Terminal (If Comfortable)

Open Terminal and run:
```bash
brew install node
```

---

## 🪟 Windows Installation

1. Go to: **nodejs.org**
2. Click the **LTS** button
3. Open the downloaded `.msi` file
4. Click "Next" through the installer
5. ✅ Make sure "Add to PATH" is checked
6. Done!

---

## ✅ Verify Installation

### Mac:
1. Open **Terminal** (search for it in Spotlight)
2. Type: `node --version`
3. Press Enter
4. You should see something like: `v20.10.0`

### Windows:
1. Open **PowerShell** or **Command Prompt**
2. Type: `node --version`
3. Press Enter
4. You should see something like: `v20.10.0`

---

## ❓ Troubleshooting

**"command not found" on Mac:**
- Close Terminal and reopen it
- Or restart your computer

**"not recognized" on Windows:**
- Close PowerShell and reopen it
- Or restart your computer

**Still not working?**
- Reinstall Node.js
- Make sure to check "Add to PATH" during installation

---

## 🎉 Great Job!

Node.js is now installed.

Next: We'll create your first MCP configuration!

steps.title: 2.3 Your First MCP Setup (File Access)
steps.content:
# Setting Up File Access MCP 📂

Let's give Claude access to a folder on your computer!

---

## Step 1: Find the Config Location

### 🍎 Mac:
The config file lives at:
```
~/Library/Application Support/Claude/claude_desktop_config.json
```

**How to open it:**
1. Open **Finder**
2. Click **Go** menu → **Go to Folder**
3. Paste: `~/Library/Application Support/Claude/`
4. Press Enter

### 🪟 Windows:
The config file lives at:
```
%APPDATA%\Claude\claude_desktop_config.json
```

**How to open it:**
1. Press **Win + R**
2. Type: `%APPDATA%\Claude`
3. Press Enter

---

## Step 2: Create a Test Folder

Create a new folder that Claude will access:

### Mac:
1. Go to your home folder
2. Create a folder called `ClaudeFiles`

### Windows:
1. Go to `C:\Users\YourName\`
2. Create a folder called `ClaudeFiles`

Put a few test files in it!

---

## Step 3: Create/Edit the Config File

Open `claude_desktop_config.json` with a text editor.

If it doesn't exist, create a new file with that name.

### 🍎 Mac Config:
```json
{
  "mcpServers": {
    "filesystem": {
      "command": "npx",
      "args": [
        "-y",
        "@modelcontextprotocol/server-filesystem",
        "/Users/YOUR_USERNAME/ClaudeFiles"
      ]
    }
  }
}
```

**Replace YOUR_USERNAME with your actual username!**

### 🪟 Windows Config:
```json
{
  "mcpServers": {
    "filesystem": {
      "command": "npx",
      "args": [
        "-y",
        "@modelcontextprotocol/server-filesystem",
        "C:/Users/YOUR_USERNAME/ClaudeFiles"
      ]
    }
  }
}
```

**Note:** Use forward slashes `/` not backslashes `\`!

---

## Step 4: Restart Claude Desktop

### Mac:
- Press ⌘ + Q to quit completely
- Reopen from Applications

### Windows:
- Right-click Claude in system tray
- Click "Exit"
- Reopen from Start Menu

---

## Step 5: Test It!

Ask Claude:
```
Can you list the files in my ClaudeFiles folder?
```

If Claude shows your files, it worked! 🎉

---

## ❓ Not Working?

**"No MCP servers found":**
- Check the config file for typos
- Make sure the path is correct
- Restart Claude Desktop

**"Permission denied":**
- Mac: System Preferences → Security → Privacy → Files → Allow Claude
- Windows: Run as Administrator once

steps.title: 2.4 Troubleshooting MCP
steps.content:
# MCP Troubleshooting Guide 🔧

## Common Issues & Fixes

### Problem 1: "MCP Server Failed to Start"

**Possible causes:**
- Node.js not installed correctly
- Wrong file path in config
- Typo in configuration

**Fixes:**
1. Verify Node.js: Open terminal, type `node --version`
2. Check your config file for typos
3. Make sure the folder path exists

---

### Problem 2: "Permission Denied" Errors

**Mac Fix:**
1. System Preferences → Security & Privacy
2. Click "Privacy" tab
3. Select "Files and Folders" in left sidebar
4. Find Claude and check the boxes

**Windows Fix:**
1. Right-click Claude Desktop
2. Select "Run as Administrator"
3. Allow access when prompted

---

### Problem 3: Claude Says "I can't access files"

**Checks:**
1. Did you restart Claude Desktop after editing config?
2. Is the folder path exactly correct?
3. Did you use the right slash direction?
   - Mac: `/Users/name/folder`
   - Windows: `C:/Users/name/folder` (forward slashes!)

---

### Problem 4: Config File Errors

**JSON Syntax Rules:**
- Every `{` needs a matching `}`
- Every `[` needs a matching `]`
- Commas between items, not after the last one
- All text must be in `"quotes"`

**Use a JSON Validator:**
1. Go to: **jsonlint.com**
2. Paste your config
3. Click "Validate"
4. It will show any errors!

---

## Quick Config Checklist ✅

- [ ] Node.js installed and working
- [ ] Config file in correct location
- [ ] JSON syntax is valid
- [ ] Folder path exists on your computer
- [ ] Using correct path format for your OS
- [ ] Claude Desktop restarted after changes
- [ ] Permissions granted (if prompted)

---

## Still Stuck?

Try this "nuclear option":

1. Delete the config file completely
2. Restart Claude Desktop
3. It should create a fresh config
4. Add your MCP servers again carefully

---

## You've Got This! 💪

MCP setup is the hardest part of this course.

Once it works, everything else is easier!

modules.title: Module 3: GitHub Integration
modules.description: Connect Claude to GitHub for code management and collaboration

steps.title: 3.1 Why Connect GitHub?
steps.content:
# GitHub + Claude = Superpowers 🐙

## What Can Claude Do with GitHub?

### Reading (Looking at code)
- 📂 List your repositories
- 📄 Read file contents
- 🔍 Search your code
- 📋 View issues and pull requests

### Writing (Making changes)
- 📝 Create new files
- ✏️ Edit existing code
- 🌿 Create branches
- 📤 Push commits
- 🔀 Create pull requests

---

## Real Examples

### Example 1: Quick Code Check
```
You: "Show me the README file from my project-x repo"

Claude: "Here's your README:
# Project X
This project is about..."
```

### Example 2: Create Something New
```
You: "Create a new file called 'utils.js' with a 
function that formats dates"

Claude: "Done! I've created utils.js with the 
formatDate function and pushed it to your repo."
```

### Example 3: Review and Fix
```
You: "What's the latest issue in my repo? Can you fix it?"

Claude: "Issue #23 is about a login bug. I can see the 
problem in auth.js line 45. Here's the fix..."
```

---

## What You'll Need

1. ✅ A GitHub account (free)
2. ✅ At least one repository (can be empty)
3. ✅ A Personal Access Token (we'll create this)

---

## Important Note ⚠️

Connecting GitHub gives Claude access to:
- Read your code
- Make changes to your repositories

**Always review changes before merging to production!**

Claude is helpful, but you're still responsible for your code.

steps.title: 3.2 Creating a GitHub Token
steps.content:
# Creating Your GitHub Token 🔑

A token is like a special password that lets Claude access GitHub.

---

## Step 1: Go to GitHub Settings

1. Log into **github.com**
2. Click your profile picture (top right)
3. Click **Settings**

---

## Step 2: Find Developer Settings

1. Scroll down the left sidebar
2. Click **Developer settings** (at the very bottom)

---

## Step 3: Create Token

1. Click **Personal access tokens**
2. Click **Tokens (classic)**
3. Click **Generate new token** → **Generate new token (classic)**

---

## Step 4: Configure Token

**Note:** Give it a name like "Claude Desktop"

**Expiration:** Choose 90 days (or No expiration if you prefer)

**Scopes (checkboxes):** Select these:
- ✅ **repo** (full control of repositories)
- ✅ **read:org** (read organization info)

---

## Step 5: Generate and SAVE!

Click **Generate token** at the bottom.

⚠️ **IMPORTANT:** Copy the token NOW!

It looks like: `ghp_xxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxx`

**You will NEVER see this token again!**

Save it somewhere safe:
- Password manager
- Secure note
- NOT in plain text on your desktop!

---

## Quick Reference

```
GitHub.com
 → Profile → Settings
   → Developer settings
     → Personal access tokens
       → Tokens (classic)
         → Generate new token (classic)
           → Select: repo, read:org
             → Generate token
               → COPY AND SAVE IT!
```

---

## ✅ You're Ready!

You now have a token.

Next: We'll add GitHub to your Claude config!

steps.title: 3.3 Connecting GitHub to Claude
steps.content:
# Adding GitHub to Your Config 🔧

## Step 1: Open Your Config File

### Mac:
```
~/Library/Application Support/Claude/claude_desktop_config.json
```

### Windows:
```
%APPDATA%\Claude\claude_desktop_config.json
```

---

## Step 2: Add GitHub Server

Your config should now look like this:

```json
{
  "mcpServers": {
    "filesystem": {
      "command": "npx",
      "args": [
        "-y",
        "@modelcontextprotocol/server-filesystem",
        "/Users/YOUR_USERNAME/ClaudeFiles"
      ]
    },
    "github": {
      "command": "npx",
      "args": [
        "-y",
        "@modelcontextprotocol/server-github"
      ],
      "env": {
        "GITHUB_PERSONAL_ACCESS_TOKEN": "ghp_YOUR_TOKEN_HERE"
      }
    }
  }
}
```

**Replace:**
- `YOUR_USERNAME` with your actual username
- `ghp_YOUR_TOKEN_HERE` with your actual GitHub token

---

## Step 3: Save and Restart

1. Save the config file
2. Quit Claude Desktop completely
3. Reopen Claude Desktop

---

## Step 4: Test It!

Ask Claude:
```
Can you list my GitHub repositories?
```

If Claude shows your repos, it worked! 🎉

---

## ❓ Common Issues

**"Bad credentials" error:**
- Check that your token is correct
- Make sure there are no extra spaces
- Token might have expired - create a new one

**"Resource not accessible":**
- Check that you selected the right scopes (repo, read:org)
- Token might need more permissions

**JSON error:**
- Check for missing commas between servers
- Validate at jsonlint.com

---

## ✅ Success!

You've connected Claude to GitHub!

Try these commands:
- "List my repositories"
- "Show me the README from [repo-name]"
- "What are the open issues in [repo-name]?"

steps.title: 3.4 GitHub Workflows with Claude
steps.content:
# Doing Real Work with GitHub 💼

## Basic Commands

### View Your Repos
```
List all my GitHub repositories
```

### Read a File
```
Show me the README.md from my [repo-name] repository
```

### Check Issues
```
What are the open issues in [repo-name]?
```

---

## Making Changes

### Create a New File
```
In my [repo-name] repository, create a new file 
called 'helpers.js' with a function that calculates age from birthdate.
```

### Edit Existing Code
```
In [repo-name], find the file main.js and add error handling 
to the login function.
```

### Create a Branch
```
Create a new branch called 'feature/user-auth' 
in my [repo-name] repository.
```

---

## Complete Workflow Example

Here's a real conversation flow:

```
You: I need to add a dark mode feature to my website repo.

Claude: I'll help! Let me first look at your current code.
[Reads relevant files]

Based on your code, I suggest:
1. Add CSS variables for colors
2. Add a toggle button
3. Save preference in localStorage

Shall I implement this?

You: Yes, please do it.

Claude: Done! I've created:
- Updated styles.css with dark mode colors
- Added toggle function in script.js
- Created a new branch 'feature/dark-mode'

Want me to create a pull request?

You: Yes.

Claude: Pull request #15 created! You can review it at:
[link to PR]
```

---

## Safety Tips ⚠️

1. **Always review** before merging to main/production
2. **Use branches** for new features
3. **Never push secrets** (API keys, passwords)
4. **Test changes** before deploying

---

## Practice Exercise 🎯

1. Create a new test repository on GitHub
2. Ask Claude to add a README file
3. Ask Claude to create an issue
4. Ask Claude to fix the issue and create a PR

This gives you safe practice before working on real projects!

modules.title: Module 4: Final Project - ClassroomNeo Integration
modules.description: Connect your Claude Desktop to ClassroomNeo and automate your learning!

steps.title: 4.1 What We're Building
steps.content:
# Your Final Project: ClassroomNeo Integration 🎓

## The Goal

Connect your Claude Desktop directly to ClassroomNeo!

This means Claude can:
- 📊 Check your course progress
- ✅ Mark steps as complete
- 📝 Submit assignments
- 📈 Track your learning

---

## Why This Matters

This project demonstrates:

1. **API Integration** - Connecting to web services
2. **Authentication** - Using API keys securely
3. **Real-world Use** - Practical automation
4. **Everything You've Learned** - MCP, config, troubleshooting

---

## What You'll Need

1. ✅ Claude Desktop working
2. ✅ MCP servers configured
3. ✅ Your ClassroomNeo student API key
4. ✅ About 30 minutes

---

## Getting Your API Key

1. Log into ClassroomNeo
2. Go to your **Profile** or **Settings**
3. Find **API Key** section
4. Copy your key

---

## How It Will Work

```
You: "What's my progress in AI Foundations?"

Claude: "Here's your progress:
- Module 1: Complete ✅
- Module 2: 75% complete
- Module 3: Not started
- Overall: 42% complete"

You: "Mark step 2.3 as complete"

Claude: "Done! Step 2.3 is now marked complete.
Your progress is now 50%."
```

---

## Ready?

Let's build this step by step!

steps.title: 4.2 Setting Up the MCP Server
steps.content:
# Connecting to ClassroomNeo 🔌

## Step 1: Get Your API Key

If you haven't already:
1. Log into ClassroomNeo
2. Go to Profile → API Settings
3. Copy your Student API Key

It looks like: `sk_student_xxxxxxxxxxxxxxxx`

---

## Step 2: Update Your Config

Open your Claude config file:

### Mac:
```
~/Library/Application Support/Claude/claude_desktop_config.json
```

### Windows:
```
%APPDATA%\Claude\claude_desktop_config.json
```

---

## Step 3: Add ClassroomNeo Server

Add this to your `mcpServers` section:

```json
"classroomneo": {
  "command": "npx",
  "args": [
    "-y", 
    "@broneotodak/classroomneo-mcp"
  ],
  "env": {
    "CLASSROOMNEO_API_KEY": "sk_student_YOUR_KEY_HERE",
    "CLASSROOMNEO_URL": "https://classroom.neotodak.com"
  }
}
```

---

## Complete Config Example

```json
{
  "mcpServers": {
    "filesystem": {
      "command": "npx",
      "args": [
        "-y",
        "@modelcontextprotocol/server-filesystem",
        "/Users/YOUR_USERNAME/ClaudeFiles"
      ]
    },
    "github": {
      "command": "npx",
      "args": ["-y", "@modelcontextprotocol/server-github"],
      "env": {
        "GITHUB_PERSONAL_ACCESS_TOKEN": "ghp_xxxxx"
      }
    },
    "classroomneo": {
      "command": "npx",
      "args": ["-y", "@broneotodak/classroomneo-mcp"],
      "env": {
        "CLASSROOMNEO_API_KEY": "sk_student_xxxxx",
        "CLASSROOMNEO_URL": "https://classroom.neotodak.com"
      }
    }
  }
}
```

---

## Step 4: Restart Claude Desktop

Completely quit and reopen Claude Desktop.

---

## Step 5: Test Connection

Ask Claude:
```
Can you check my progress in ClassroomNeo?
```

If you see your progress, you're connected! 🎉

steps.title: 4.3 Using the Integration
steps.content:
# Working with ClassroomNeo 📚

## Available Commands

### Check Your Progress
```
"What's my progress in ClassroomNeo?"
"How far am I in the AI Foundations course?"
"Which modules have I completed?"
```

### Get Course Information
```
"What courses am I enrolled in?"
"What's the next step I should work on?"
"Show me the curriculum for Claude Desktop Mastery"
```

### Update Progress
```
"Mark step 2.1 as complete"
"I finished module 1, update my progress"
```

### Submit Assignments
```
"Submit my screenshot for step 1.2"
"I completed the assignment, here's my work: [description]"
```

### Get Help
```
"I'm stuck on step 3.2, can you help?"
"Explain the assignment for the current step"
"What do I need to do next?"
```

---

## Power User Workflow

Combine what you've learned:

```
You: I want to work on my ClassroomNeo assignments today.
     Show me where I left off and what I need to do next.

Claude: You're at Step 2.3 in AI Foundations.
This step is about "The Art of Follow-Up Questions."

The assignment asks you to:
1. Have a conversation with me
2. Use at least 3 follow-up questions
3. Screenshot the improvement from first to final answer

Ready to start?

You: Yes, let's do it!

Claude: Great! What topic should we practice with?

[...conversation happens...]

You: That was great! Please submit my work and mark the step complete.

Claude: Done! I've:
✅ Marked step 2.3 as complete
📝 Submitted your assignment

You're now 75% through Module 2!
```

---

## Tips for Success

1. **Be specific** about which course/step
2. **Check progress** before starting each session
3. **Submit as you go** - don't wait until the end
4. **Ask for help** - Claude can explain any step

steps.title: 4.4 Congratulations! 🎉
steps.content:
# You Did It! 🎓🎉

## What You've Accomplished

You've gone from AI beginner to Claude Desktop power user!

### ✅ Skills Mastered:

1. **Claude Desktop Installation**
   - Downloaded and set up the app
   - Learned the interface

2. **MCP Configuration**
   - Installed Node.js
   - Created config files
   - Troubleshot issues

3. **File System Integration**
   - Claude can read your files
   - Work with documents locally

4. **GitHub Integration**
   - Created access tokens
   - Connected repositories
   - Managed code with Claude

5. **ClassroomNeo Integration**
   - API key setup
   - Progress tracking
   - Automated learning!

---

## You're Now a Claude Power User! 🦸

You can:
- 🗂️ Work with files on your computer
- 🐙 Manage GitHub repositories
- 📊 Track your learning automatically
